response:
# Review of review-01-messy-ts-app

Reviewing this as a fresh PR against an empty baseline, using the skill's decision rule (a new `severity: "high"` finding is a blocker unless the rationale for leaving it is explicit) and `scores.agent_risk` as the tiebreaker on mediums.

37 findings ÷ 7 block / 22 warn / 8 ignore. PR is not mergeable as-is — the date module and the two "works-on-my-machine" literals are the load-bearing problems; the rest is debt to file rather than gate on.

---

## BLOCK MERGE (7)

These are either `severity: "high"` or `severity: "medium"` correctness bugs that fail outside the author's laptop. They have to be addressed in this PR or explicitly punted by the author.

| id | charge | sev / ar | why block |
|---|---|---|---|
| **crime_00002** | God Function `generateInvoice` | 0.9 / 0.63 | 204 lines (3.4× the 60-line domain threshold) in *billing* code. Per the skill's auto-fix policy this can't be silently split, but landing 204 lines of mixed-responsibility billing logic is the canonical case the policy is trying to prevent — author must split or justify. |
| **crime_00003** | Temporal Recklessness in `src/date.ts` | 0.8 / 0.69 | 8 direct `Date.now()` / `new Date()` calls (`lines: 4, 8, 13, 17, 22, 29, 33, 40`). This *is* the date module — every consumer is non-deterministic. Highest `agent_risk` in the report. |
| **crime_00004** | Half-UTC, Half-Local | 0.8 / 0.68 | Same `d` receiver uses `getUTCFullYear()` @L9 and `getMonth()` @L41. Silent host-offset bugs — the kind that ship green and break a day before launch. |
| **crime_00001** | SVG With Embedded Raster | 0.75 / 0.7 | `partner-logo.svg` carries 2 base64 rasters (`image/jpeg`, `image/png`). High confidence (0.95) and an obvious asset-pipeline fix — re-export from the design tool. Not a code change; should be done before merge rather than waved through. |
| **crime_00006** | Dev-Server URL | 0.6 / 0.61 | `localhost:3000` hardcoded in `src/api/files.ts:8`. Medium severity in the schema, but it's a pure portability bug — this PR cannot run in CI or staging. |
| **crime_00007** | Localhost-on-Disk | 0.6 / 0.61 | `/Users/andrew/dev/crimes/schema.json` hardcoded at `src/api/files.ts:9`. Works on exactly one machine. Same reasoning as 00006 — medium severity by the rubric, but a hard blocker by reality. |
| **crime_00010** | Timezone Roulette | 0.6 / 0.61 | `new Date("2026-12-25T07:00:00")` and `new Date("2026-12-20")` in `src/date.ts:29,33` — naked literals get whatever the host TZ feels like. Pairs with 00003 and 00004; the entire date module needs to land together. |

---

## WARN (22)

Real findings I'd block on a follow-up PR but not on first introduction. Grouping by theme; cite each id.

**Date-module cleanup beyond the highs** — same module, smaller surfaces. Fix once the high-severity rewrite lands.
- **crime_00009** (Temporal Recklessness, billing.ts, 0.6 / 0.53) — 7 more direct dates outside the date module; should consume whatever clock abstraction the highs introduce.
- **crime_00014** (DST-Naive Day Math, 0.6 / 0.59) — `+ 86400000` at `src/date.ts:52`. Drifts on DST transitions.
- **crime_00024** (Temporal Recklessness, state.ts, 0.35 / 0.51) — one stray `Date.now()` at L18.
- **crime_00026** (Date String Sewing, 0.3 / 0.5) — manual `.getUTCFullYear()` concat at L41.
- **crime_00027** (Host-Locale Drift, 0.3 / 0.5) — `toLocaleDateString()` with no locale arg @L46.

**Identity / source-of-truth drift** — every one of these is `agent_risk ≥ 0.56` because they mislead readers (human or LLM).
- **crime_00012** (String Sprinkles, 0.5 / 0.6) — `"enterprise"` in 4 production files, no centralised constant.
- **crime_00013** (False Identity, 0.55 / 0.52) — `calculateRefundPreview` calls `saveRefundAudit` + `sendRefundEmail`. Misleading name in a destructive code path.
- **crime_00015** (Concept Alias Drift, 0.55 / 0.58) — *tenant / account / organisation / team / workspace* used interchangeably across 5 files.
- **crime_00017** (Logic in the Alibi, 0.5 / 0.5) — `billing.ts:39` comment "Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves." isn't encoded in nearby code. This is the rule for the refund function — surface as a guard.
- **crime_00018** (Duplicated Navigation Source, 0.6 / 0.57) — `/settings/billing` is "Plans" in registry, "Billing" in sidebar.
- **crime_00020** (Route Metadata Drift, 0.6 / 0.56) — same `/settings/billing` is labelled 4 different ways across path/component/title/nav.

**Structural / agent-context** — debt the next agent edit will trip over.
- **crime_00005** (Missing Agent Context, 0.6 / 0.61) — no AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / skill. Block-adjacent in an agent-native repo, but not a correctness gate.
- **crime_00016** (God File, 0.6 / 0.5) — `billing.ts` 310 lines (just over the 300 threshold). Will resolve when 00002 is split.
- **crime_00019** (Unfinished Business, 0.4 / 0.57) — 14 TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX in `todo.ts` (424/kLOC). Author needs to triage what's load-bearing vs vestigial before this lands; flagging rather than blocking because the file may itself be a stub.

**Test signal**
- **crime_00011** (Test That Proves Nothing, 0.45 / 0.46) — "renders the enterprise plan" has 0 expects. Misleading green check.
- **crime_00030** (Test That Proves Nothing, 0.25 / 0.34) — "creates an invoice" only uses weak matchers. Lower agent_risk but same class of problem.

**Performance / hygiene**
- **crime_00021** (Oversized Raster, 0.4 / 0.52) — 254 KB PNG. Easy follow-up.
- **crime_00023** (Sync I/O in Hot Path, 0.4 / 0.51) — 3 `*Sync` calls in `loadPlanSchema`. Async variants exist; in an API hot path this matters but isn't a correctness gate.

**Documentation / navigation**
- **crime_00022** (Docs-Code Drift, 0.35 / 0.51) — `docs/teams.md:5` links to missing `./setup.md`.
- **crime_00036**, **crime_00037**, **crime_00038** (Orphaned Destination ×3, 0.4 / 0.46) — `/account/subscription`, `/team`, `/workspace/members` unreachable from any nav, doc, or import. Likely the artefact of the alias-drift problem (00015) — could be in-flight pages or could be dead. Author should mark each.

**API surface**
- **crime_00031** (Option Bag Junk Drawer, 0.3 / 0.48) — `describeCheckout({options})` reads 6 distinct properties without a named shape.
- **crime_00032** (Return Shape Roulette, 0.3 / 0.48) — `resolvePaymentResult` returns 3 disjoint shapes, no annotated type. Quote: *"lowest key overlap: 0%"*.

---

## IGNORE (8)

Stylistic or already-implied-by-other-findings. Not worth a PR comment.

- **crime_00025** (Icon-Sized Raster, 0.35 / 0.5) — 32×32 PNG. Asset choice, not risk.
- **crime_00028** / **crime_00029** (Unprefixed Boolean ×2, 0.3 / 0.49) — `paid`/`expired`/`stale` and `disableBilling`/`skipRetry`. Naming preference; detector even hints at `allowedNames` config if the project disagrees.
- **crime_00033** (Negative Flag Maze, 0.25 / 0.47) — `!disableBilling && !skipRetry`. Style; consumed by 00029 if the booleans get renamed.
- **crime_00034** (Plural Mismatch, 0.3 / 0.47) — `users: User` / `invoice: Invoice[]`. Could be a real typo, but v1 detector that "silently skips type aliases" — accept the false-positive risk on low-severity items.
- **crime_00035** (Unfinished Business, billing.ts, 0.2 / 0.4) — only 12.9/kLOC in `billing.ts`. The bigger TODO problem (00019) covers the policy decision.

The 0.7.0 skill guidance applies: I'd recommend `crimes feedback <fingerprint> --verdict fp --note "<reason>"` for any of these the author actively disagrees with, rather than tightening detector thresholds in one PR.

---

## Summary line for the author

> Blocking on 4 highs + 3 mediums (date module rewrite, two hardcoded environment paths, one billing god-function, one SVG with embedded rasters). Date module is the load-bearing fix — 00003 / 00004 / 00010 / 00009 / 00014 should land together. Everything else is debt I'd take as follow-up tickets, not gate this PR on.
